entries.rationale: The ABI shell phase does not build the original upstream C implementation that these quilt patches modified; the safe source replaces that codepath with Rust-exported ABI placeholders and a package-surface shell.

The ABI shell phase does not ship or execute the original desktop-file integration logic that these Debian patches adjusted, so carrying them as an active quilt queue would not apply to the safe source tree.

The ABI shell phase does not ship or execute the original desktop-file integration logic that these Debian patches adjusted, so carrying them as an active quilt queue would not apply to the safe source tree.

The Debian-only packaging and test-workaround behaviour is implemented directly in the safe packaging, copied autopkgtests, and package harness instead of reapplying the upstream quilt patch to a non-upstream source tree.
